title: PCI-DSS 12.3 — Targeted risk analysis: frequency adherence for per-requirement TRAs
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the TRA content; reports TRAs whose refresh is overdue per documented frequency.
evidence: Saved search 'pci_12_3_tra_refresh' (daily) emits per TRA: tra_id, requirement, frequency_days, last_refresh_at, overdue_days, to compliance_summary.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A TRA with frequency_days=365 and last_refresh_at 400d ago returns overdue_days=35.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A TRA refreshed in the past 200d with frequency_days=365 is absent.

spl:
| inputlookup pci_tra_register.csv
| join type=outer tra_id
  [search index=grc sourcetype=archer:tra_record earliest=-2y
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_refresh_at BY tra_id]
| eval overdue_days=round((now()-last_refresh_at)/86400,0)-frequency_days
| where overdue_days>0 OR isnull(last_refresh_at)
| table tra_id requirement frequency_days last_refresh_at overdue_days
description: PCI-DSS v4.0 introduced targeted risk analyses (TRAs) at 12.3. This UC makes the refresh cadence measurable.
value: Gives compliance an explicit overdue list — the v4.0 TRA is a common reason for 'in progress' at year-two assessments.
implementation: (1) Maintain pci_tra_register.csv with requirement and frequency_days; (2) GRC writes each refresh event; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) overdue TRAs open a CISO-owned task; (5) quarterly compliance report.
visualization: Table of overdue TRAs, bar chart by requirement, single value 'TRAs overdue'.
knownFalsePositives: TRAs whose requirement is marked 'not applicable' should be excluded via scope_applicable=false.
grandmaExplanation: Gives compliance an explicit overdue list — the v4.0 TRA is a common reason for 'in progress' at year-two assessments — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` sourcetype=archer:tra_record; TRA register `pci_tra_register.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain pci_tra_register.csv with requirement and frequency_days; (2) GRC writes each refresh event; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) overdue TRAs open a CISO-owned task; (5) quarterly compliance report.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup pci_tra_register.csv
| join type=outer tra_id
  [search index=grc sourcetype=archer:tra_record earliest=-2y
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_refresh_at BY tra_id]
| eval overdue_days=round((now()-last_refresh_at)/86400,0)-frequency_days
| where overdue_days>0 OR isnull(last_refresh_at)
| table tra_id requirement frequency_days last_refresh_at overdue_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**PCI-DSS 12.3 — Targeted risk analysis: frequency adherence for per-requirement TRAs** — Gives compliance an explicit overdue list — the v4.0 TRA is a common reason for 'in progress' at year-two assessments.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` sourcetype=archer:tra_record; TRA register `pci_tra_register.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **overdue_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where overdue_days>0 OR isnull(last_refresh_at)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **PCI-DSS 12.3 — Targeted risk analysis: frequency adherence for per-requirement TRAs**): table tra_id requirement frequency_days last_refresh_at overdue_days


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of overdue TRAs, bar chart by requirement, single value 'TRAs overdue'.
